name: Chile-Rubbed Pork Tenderloin
recipeIngredient: 7 pounds pork tenderloin, cut into 9 pieces
recipeInstructions:
1. Poach lemon zest and orange zest in water until tender.
2. Strain and set aside.
3. Sauté julienned red onion in vegetable oil over medium heat until tender.
4. Continue cooking the onions over high heat until they begin to caramelize.
5. Deglaze the pan with port wine, reducing the heat.
6. Add dried Michigan cherries, lemon juice, orange juice, grated ginger, chopped jalapeno chile, cayenne pepper, and a pinch of nutmeg.
7. Reduce the mixture to a syrup consistency and add salt to taste.
8. Stir in reserved lemon zest and green onion.
9. Season pork tenderloin pieces with chili rub (1 tablespoon chili powder, 1 teaspoon paprika, 1 teaspoon granulated onion or garlic powder, and 1 teaspoon salt).
10. Place pork 3 inches from the flame on a charcoal grill or under a commercial broiler.
11. Sear all sides of the pork until an internal temperature of 150-155 degrees F is reached, about 6-8 minutes total.
12. Remove pork from heat and let it rest for 5 minutes before slicing.
Serving suggestions: Plate pork with cider bourbon sauce and dried cherry chutney. Serve with seasonal vegetables.
